comment.name: Comment
comment.text:
Afternoon Report

Last Updated: 3/18/2026 | 12:46PM


Good afternoon, Sunday River!

It's pretty obvious how much work the grooming team put in last night to get the mountain into great shape. This morning's turns have been smooth, crisp, and holding edges really well. With favorable temps again tonight, the team will be back out doing the same. In the meantime, click in, soak up the sun, and take advantage of the excellent surface conditions.

If you haven't checked the events calendar lately, now's the time. The upcoming stretch is stacked with classics like Party in the Park, BFF Day, Bust N' Burn, Pond-a-Palooza, and plenty more.

Lastly, the 2026/27 New England Pass is on sale now, and for a limited time, it's at its lowest price of the year. Lock it in today, start planning next winter, and keep skiing this season at no extra cost.

We'll see you out there!

-Team Snow

comment.name: Comment 2
comment.text:
Good morning, Sunday River!

If you caught that sunrise, you already know it's a good one. Clear skies, crisp air, and a mountain that looks ready to go. Temps are starting cold with 4° on Locke, 6° at North Peak, 8° in Jordan, and 13° at White Cap, then climbing into the low 20s up top and upper 20s down low under partly to mostly sunny skies.

The grooming team took full advantage of those overnight temps and left things in great shape. Expect smooth, consistent corduroy to start, with soft snow that's perfect for laying into your edges right out of the gate.

There is a bit of wind to start the morning, especially up top, and that could play with lift operations early. The good news is it's expected to settle down pretty quickly as we move through the day, so things should open up nicely after the initial push.

Heads up over on T2 and Monday Morning, those trails are closed for the U14 Super G Championships. If you're moving through Locke, there will be crossing guards posted to keep things flowing and get you where you need to go without issue.

Beyond today, the forecast is lining up for a really solid stretch. If you've been waiting for a window to stack some quality laps, this is it.

Time to make your move on the 2026/27 New England Pass. If you had one this season, lock it back in and keep things rolling, and if you didn't, you can still jump in at the lowest price of the year and start skiing right now for the rest of this season. Either way, buying now is how you squeeze the most value out of it.

We'll see you out there!

-Team Snow
